summary: Matched the first capsule item state table, it_803F5468, in C as an ItemStateTable array and corrected related callback return types. To preserve the surrounding data layout, the large unmatched data tail that formerly lived after it_803F5468 in asm/melee/it/it_803F1418.s was moved into a new asm object, asm/melee/it/it_803F5468.s, and obj_files.mk was updated to link that new object immediately after src/melee/it/itcapsule.c.o. There were no PR or review comments in the slice.

key_files.role: Added the matched C definition for ItemStateTable it_803F5468 and changed it_8027D30C to return bool by forwarding it_802751D8.
Updated it_8027D30C prototype from void to bool.
Removed the raw .4byte data block beginning at it_803F5468 from the older asm data file.
New asm data file holding the remaining unmatched data symbols after the now-matched it_803F5468 table, starting at it_803F54D8.
Added $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/it_803F5468.s.o after src/melee/it/itcapsule.c.o to preserve object/data ordering.

what_changed: Converted the raw assembly table at it_803F5468 into a C ItemStateTable array in src/melee/it/itcapsule.c.

The matched table contains seven four-field entries using state IDs such as -1, 0, and 1, callback function pointers such as it_8027D110, it_8027D118, it_8027D11C, and NULL where the raw asm used NULL pointers.
Changed the local declaration of it_802751D8 from void it_802751D8(Item_GObj*) to bool it_802751D8(Item_GObj*).
Changed it_8027D30C from a void wrapper into a bool-returning wrapper: it now returns it_802751D8(item_gobj).
Updated src/melee/it/itcapsule.h so it_8027D30C is declared as bool.
Removed approximately 4215 lines of data from asm/melee/it/it_803F1418.s and reintroduced most of that data in a new asm/melee/it/it_803F5468.s file, excluding the now-matched it_803F5468 table.
Added asm/melee/it/it_803F5468.s.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk directly after itcapsule.c.o.
smart_moves: Matched only the table with enough type evidence instead of trying to convert the entire large data region at once.

Preserved link order by inserting the new asm object immediately after itcapsule.c.o, allowing the compiled C definition of it_803F5468 to occupy the old slot while later data symbols remain available from assembly.
Used the project’s ItemStateTable type rather than leaving the table as anonymous .4byte values, making the repeated four-word layout explicit.
Corrected callback return types to bool so the C table function-pointer fields and wrapper behavior matched the data-table semantics.
Kept address-based symbol names for unresolved functions and globals, avoiding speculative naming.
decomp_lessons: For large contiguous .data blobs, a practical matching tactic is to peel off one matched table into C and move the still-unmatched tail into a new asm file that follows the C object in obj_files.mk.
When a state table entry points at a wrapper function, the wrapper’s return type must agree with the table callback type; here a void wrapper had to become bool and return the callee result.
Raw 0xFFFFFFFF state IDs in assembly can correspond to -1 in typed C initializers when matching signed state-table fields.
Keep NULL entries as explicit NULL in C table initializers rather than numeric zeroes for pointer fields.
Object ordering is part of data matching: adding a separate asm file is not sufficient unless the new object is placed where the old data tail needs to link.
naming_conventions: Kept unresolved data/global symbol names in address form, including it_803F5468 and it_803F54D8.
Kept unresolved callback function names in address form, such as it_8027D110, it_8027D118, it_8027D11C, it_8027D30C, it_8027D32C, and it_8027D330.
Used the existing project type name ItemStateTable for item state-table data.
Used bool for state callback functions that return success/transition status, as shown by it_802751D8 and it_8027D30C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split a large asm data range: removed it_803F5468 and following symbols from asm/melee/it/it_803F1418.s, then created asm/melee/it/it_803F5468.s for the remaining unmatched data.
The new asm file starts with .include "macros.inc", .section .data, and .balign 8 before the first remaining global, preserving data-section structure.
Inserted the new asm object after the C object in obj_files.mk, which is essential for preserving symbol addresses after moving the first table into C.
Represented the matched table as structured C initializers, grouping each old four-.4byte row into one ItemStateTable entry.
Converted 0xFFFFFFFF assembly entries to -1 in C for signed table fields and retained NULL for absent function pointers.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert additional tables from asm/melee/it/it_803F5468.s into C ItemStateTable definitions.
Search for other item state tables where a void callback wrapper should return bool.
Look for obj_files.mk changes that place an asm tail object after a newly matched C data object.
Audit remaining raw .4byte state-table blobs for four-field ItemStateTable patterns with -1/NULL/function-pointer entries.
